{{Quote|No. They are not mythical. I wish they were. I hoped never to see one. The Emperor caused a few to be created. He gave them as rewards, to the cruelest and most loyal of his officers. Tokens, he called them. His "tokens" were a greater gift than a natural world.|[[Rillao]], on the worldcraft|<ref name="CS" />}}
A '''Worldcraft''' was a large type of [[habitation sphere]].<ref name="DOAE2" /> The Worldcraft were artificial planetoids which resembled a terrestrial planet or moon and were capable of traveling through [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] and realspace.<ref name="CS" />

==Characteristics==
The mobile planetoid had real terrain including a forested surface with several small bodies of water including rivers and swamps, an unknown stretch of desert and a blue "sky". The Worldcraft retained its atmosphere through the use of a [[Force field/Legends|force field]] while buried [[gravfield plate]]s maintain the standard gravity of the worldcraft. The core of the Worldcraft was an immense reactor that powered the [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] engines and the [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]], the latter used to hold a miniature "sun" in place. Due to its miniature size and its rapid rotation on its axis, days on the Worldcraft were much shorter than standard days on most other worlds.<ref name="CS" />

The former Imperial [[Procurator of Justice]] [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] [[Hethrir]], who owned a Worldcraft, maintained a small compound on his station which contained a "school" for children he kidnapped, living quarters, a dinner house, training quarters and a control center for the planetoid. It appears the Worldcraft was largely automated and required no organic crew to pilot it.<ref name="CS" />

A Worldcraft could be adapted for different purposes, including being turned into a [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]] or a repair station for starfleets.<ref name="DOAE2" />

==History==
The only known manufacturer of this class of starships was the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. There were only a few vessels in existence, each commissioned by [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] himself as a gift to his most loyal and capable servants.<ref name="TEGTC">''[[The Essential Guide to Characters]]''</ref> The Procurator of Justice Lord Hethrir was granted his Worldcraft by Emperor Palpatine after he committed genocide against his own species, the [[Firrerreo/Legends|Firrerreo]]. Hethrir named his gift the ''[[Rebirth]]''<ref name="AitE">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/insideronline/96/indexp2.html|series=Aliens in the Empire|text=Part 1: Hierarchy of Power}}</ref> and used it as a mobile base for his "[[Empire Reborn]] movement". After his death, the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] took possession of the station.<ref name="CS" />

[[File:Habitation_spheres.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Two Worldcraft being constructed over [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].]]
At the height of the Galactic Empire, the operative [[Ennix Devian]], alongside Imperial [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Ardus Kaine]], were also intended to be recipients of a Worldcraft each, although Palpatine died before the Worldcraft could be completed. Devian stole both stations after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] (being especially delighted to learn that his rival Kaine was intended to be a recipient) and sent the two to opposite parts of the galaxy. [[Death Star III|One]] went to the Moddell Sector, accompanied by a fleet of [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]] and the other to the Spawn Nebula. The station sent to [[Endor/Legends|Endor]] was fitted to be a mock-up of a [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]] battlestation, as part of Devian's ruse. The similarities between the Worldcraft and the Death Stars were enough to fool [[Alliance of Free Planets]] forces and the station kept the Alliance occupied in the [[Battle of Death Star III]], while Devian's Dissension forces raided Alliance [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyards]] in neighboring [[Star system/Legends|star systems]].<ref name="DOAE2" />

Devian's surviving Worldcraft was kept in secret in the Spawn Nebula and used to repair and refurbish a fleet of [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]]. Among the growing fleet were warships refitted with old [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederate]] technology in the form of powerful [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannons]], derived from the [[Subjugator-class heavy cruiser/Legends|''Subjugator''-class heavy cruisers]]. The repair station and much of the fleet were later destroyed during an [[Mission to the Spawn Nebula|attack]] by [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] forces, using unstable [[Illerium]] brought aboard the Worldcraft by saboteurs.<ref name="DOAE2" />
